name: Typeclasses.Classes.Comparison

aliases.name: Comparison
aliases.comment: Explicit typeclass which implements comparison operations for type `a`.
aliases.type: { equality : Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality a, compare : a -> a -> Basics.Order, lt : a -> a -> Basics.Bool, le : a -> a -> Basics.Bool, gt : a -> a -> Basics.Bool, ge : a -> a -> Basics.Bool, min : a -> a -> a, max : a -> a -> a }

values.name: comparable
values.comment: Construct an instance for a comparable type using the compiler-generated definitions.

values.name: compare
values.comment: Construct an instance from just a `compare` function.

values.name: equalityAndLt
values.comment:
Construct an instance from an instance of equality and
a lesser-than function.
values.type: Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality a -> (a -> a -> Basics.Bool) -> Typeclasses.Classes.Comparison.Comparison a

values.name: float
values.comment: Instance for `Float`.

values.name: int
values.comment: Instance for `Int`.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Map over the owner type of an instance to produce a new instance.

You need to provide both a covariant and a contravariant mapping
(i.e., `(a -> b)` and `(b -> a)`).
values.type: (a -> b) -> (b -> a) -> Typeclasses.Classes.Comparison.Comparison a -> Typeclasses.Classes.Comparison.Comparison b

name: Typeclasses.Classes.Equality

aliases.name: Equality
aliases.comment: Explicit typeclass which implements equality for type `a`.
aliases.type: { eq : a -> a -> Basics.Bool, notEq : a -> a -> Basics.Bool }

values.name: comparable
values.comment: Instance for any type, which satisfies the magic comparable constraint.

values.name: compare
values.comment: Construct from a comparison operation.

values.name: either
values.comment: Instance for Either, with instances for its members provided.
values.type: Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality a -> Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality b -> Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality (Either.Either a b)

values.name: eq
values.comment: Construct just from the eq operation.

values.name: eqAndNotEq
values.comment: Construct from the eq and not-eq operations.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Bool) -> (a -> a -> Basics.Bool) -> Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality a

values.name: float
values.comment: Instance for Float.

values.name: int
values.comment: Instance for Int.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Map over the owner type of an instance to produce a new instance.

Please notice that mapping is contravariant (i.e., `(b -> a)` instead of `(a -> b)`).

values.name: tuple2
values.comment: Instance for tuple, with instances for its members provided.
values.type: Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality a -> Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality b -> Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality ( a, b )

values.name: tuple3
values.comment: Instance for tuple, with instances for its members provided.
values.type: Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality a -> Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality b -> Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality c -> Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality ( a, b, c )

name: Typeclasses.Classes.Hashing

aliases.name: Hashing
aliases.comment: Explicit typeclass which implements hashing for type `a`.
aliases.type: { hash : a -> Basics.Int, hashWithSalt : Basics.Int -> a -> Basics.Int }

values.name: array
values.comment:
Hashing instance for arrays,
determined by the Hashing instance for elements and sampling.
Sampling determines the maximum amount of elements to be picked
from the array while constructing the hash,
allowing you to control the balance between the quality of hashing and its performance.

values.name: hash
values.comment: Construct from just the hashing function.

values.name: hashWithSalt
values.comment: Construct from just the hashing function, which combines the hash with salt.

values.name: int
values.comment: Instance for `Int`.

values.name: list
values.comment:
Instance for `List`, which utilizes an instance for its element.
Traverses the list in whole.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Map over the owner type of an instance to produce a new instance.

Please notice that mapping is contravariant (i.e., `(b -> a)` instead of `(a -> b)`).

values.name: string
values.comment:
Hashing instance for strings, determined by sampling.
Sampling determines the maximum amount of characters to be picked
from the string while constructing the hash,
allowing you to control the balance between the quality of hashing and its performance.

name: Typeclasses.Classes.Monoid

aliases.name: Monoid
aliases.comment: Explicit typeclass which implements monoid operations for type `a`.
aliases.type: { semigroup : Typeclasses.Classes.Semigroup.Semigroup a, identity : a, concat : List.List a -> a }

values.name: appendable
values.comment:
Construct an instance for any type which satisfies Elm's `appendable` magic constraint,
by providing an identity value.

values.name: cmd
values.comment: Instance for commands under the batch operation.

values.name: identityAndConcat
values.comment: Construct an instance by specifying identity value and a concatenation operation.

values.name: intProduct
values.comment: Instance for integers under the multiplication operation.

values.name: intSum
values.comment: Instance for integers under the sum operation.

values.name: list
values.comment: Instance for list under concatenation.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Map over the owner type of an instance to produce a new instance.

You need to provide both a covariant and a contravariant mapping
(i.e., `(a -> b)` and `(b -> a)`).
values.type: (a -> b) -> (b -> a) -> Typeclasses.Classes.Monoid.Monoid a -> Typeclasses.Classes.Monoid.Monoid b

values.name: maybeFirst
values.comment: Instance for maybe, which chooses the first `Just` value.

values.name: numberProduct
values.comment:
Construct an instance for any type which satisfies Elm's `number` magic constraint.
Implements multiplication.

values.name: numberSum
values.comment:
Construct an instance for any type which satisfies Elm's `number` magic constraint.
Implements sum.

values.name: semigroupAndIdentity
values.comment: Construct an instance by specifying a semigroup instance and an identity value.

values.name: setDifference
values.comment: Instance for set under the difference operation.

values.name: setUnion
values.comment: Instance for set under the union operation.

values.name: string
values.comment: Instance for strings under the appending operation.

values.name: sub
values.comment: Instance for subscriptions under the batch operation.

values.name: task
values.comment: Instance for tasks, which sequentially executes them and groups the results.

name: Typeclasses.Classes.Semigroup

aliases.name: Semigroup
aliases.comment:
Explicit typeclass which implements semigroup operations for type `a`.

Notice that the binary operation function is named "prepend" instead of "append",
because it follows the convention of having the context value come as the last value.

values.name: appendable
values.comment: Construct an instance for any type which satisfies Elm's `appendable` magic constraint.

values.name: cmd
values.comment: Instance for commands under the batch operation.

values.name: concat
values.comment: Construct from a concatenation function.

values.name: intProduct
values.comment: Instance for integers under the multiplication operation.

values.name: intSum
values.comment: Instance for integers under the sum operation.

values.name: list
values.comment: Instance for list under concatenation.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Map over the owner type of an instance to produce a new instance.

You need to provide both a covariant and a contravariant mapping
(i.e., `(a -> b)` and `(b -> a)`).
values.type: (a -> b) -> (b -> a) -> Typeclasses.Classes.Semigroup.Semigroup a -> Typeclasses.Classes.Semigroup.Semigroup b

values.name: maybeFirst
values.comment: Instance for maybe, which chooses the first `Just` value.

values.name: numberProduct
values.comment:
Construct an instance for any type which satisfies Elm's `number` magic constraint.
Implements multiplication.

values.name: numberSum
values.comment:
Construct an instance for any type which satisfies Elm's `number` magic constraint.
Implements sum.

values.name: prepend
values.comment: Construct from a prepend function.

values.name: setDifference
values.comment: Instance for set under the difference operation.

values.name: setIntersection
values.comment: Instance for set under the intersection operation.

values.name: setUnion
values.comment: Instance for set under the union operation.

values.name: string
values.comment: Instance for strings under the appending operation.

values.name: sub
values.comment: Instance for subscriptions under the batch operation.

values.name: task
values.comment: Instance for tasks, which sequentially executes them and groups the results.

name: Typeclasses.Extensions.List
comment:
Extensions to the list API, which utilise typeclasses.

@docs fold, foldMap, sort, sortBy, member, dedupe

values.name: dedupe
values.comment: *O(n^2)*. Deduplicate a list using an instance of `Equality`.
values.type: Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality a -> List.List a -> List.List a

values.name: fold
values.comment: *O(n)*. Combine the elements of list using a `Monoid` instance.

values.name: foldMap
values.comment:
*O(n)*. Map each element of the list to a type which has an instance of `Monoid`,
and combine the results.
values.type: Typeclasses.Classes.Monoid.Monoid b -> (a -> b) -> List.List a -> b

values.name: member
values.comment: *O(n)*. Check whether a value is a member of a list based on the value's `Equality` instance.
values.type: Typeclasses.Classes.Equality.Equality a -> a -> List.List a -> Basics.Bool

values.name: sort
values.comment: Sort the elements using a `Comparison` instance.
values.type: Typeclasses.Classes.Comparison.Comparison a -> List.List a -> List.List a

values.name: sortBy
values.comment: Sort the elements by mapping to a type which has an instance of `Comparison`.
values.type: Typeclasses.Classes.Comparison.Comparison b -> (a -> b) -> List.List a -> List.List a
